name: German Chocolate Brownie Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place sheets of foil on countertop for cooling cookies.
2. In a large bowl, combine brown sugar, shortening, water, and vanilla. Beat at medium speed of electric mixer until well blended. Beat in eggs one at a time.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa, salt, and baking soda. Mix into the creamed mixture at low speed just until blended. Stir in chocolate chips.
4. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls (15 ml) of dough 2 inches (5 cm) apart onto ungreased baking sheets.
5. Bake one sheet at a time for 7 to 9 minutes, or until cookies are set and lightly golden around the edges. Do not overbake. Cool on baking sheets for 2 minutes before removing to foil to cool completely.
6. For topping, combine evaporated milk, sugar, shortening, and egg yolks in medium saucepan. Stir over medium heat until thickened. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla, pecans, and coconut. Allow topping to cool completely. Frost cooled cookies.
